title: Hanging Storage Shelf
summary: This hanging storage shelf is perfect for your entryway, mudroom or bathroom.
description: This hanging storage shelf is perfect for your entryway, mudroom or bathroom. Featuring a nice shelf for decor, hooks for hanging, and three storage bins for keeping bills, mail, supplies, etc. Not only will it be a beautiful addition to your home but it will keep you organized at the same time!

cutList.part: Sides of face frame for front of bin
Top and bottom of face frame for front of bin

steps.title: Cut the sides
steps.body: For the two sides you will need to cut two 16'' long pieces from a 1x12 board. Then, using a Kreg Rip cut or table saw, rip the two boards to 10''. This will give you two 10''x16'' boards. Referring to the photo, measure and mark on your boards where the semi circles start by using the dimensions given. Using a circular object, like a bowl, trace a semi circle connecting the marks. Cut out semi circles with a jigsaw and then sand smooth.

steps.title: Attach back pieces
steps.body: For the back of the shelf you will need a 1x12 and a 1x6 board. Cut both boards to 29 1/4'' long. Rip your 1x6 board so that it is 4 3/4'' wide. Then using glue and pocket hole screws attach your 1x12 and ripped 1x6 board together to create the 29 1/4''x16'' back piece.

steps.title: Join back and sides together
steps.body: Using glue and pocket hole screws attach the back to the two side pieces.

steps.title: Add top shelf
steps.body: With glue and pocket hole screws attach the top shelf to the sides and back of the shelf.

steps.title: Connecting dividers
steps.body: With the shelf upside down attach the shelf dividers with glue and pocket hole screws to the bottom of the top shelf.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Glue and screw on the bottom shelf to the sides and back, and attach dividers with finish nails.

steps.title: Building storage bins
steps.body: I used scrap 3/4'' plywood for my storage bins, because I needed to use it up. You can use 3/4''or 1/2'' plywood or any scrap wood to build these bins. Using glue and 2'' finish nails attach the two sides to the bottom.

steps.title: Attach back of bin
steps.body: Attach the back of the bin with glue and finish nails to the sides and bottom. Pictured is an optional front piece if you do not want to do the front that is in the next step.

steps.title: Make open front for bins
steps.body: Using 1x2's and 3/4'' pocket holes and 1 1/4'' pocket hole screws and glue, create a face frame for the open front of the bin. Attach hardware cloth to the back of the frame with staples. Then glue and nail the open front with hardware cloth onto the sides and bottom of the bin. Add knobs to each bin.

steps.title: Finish as desired
steps.body: I choose to stain my project with special walnut, and then finished with three coats of poly. Once you finish painting or staining, add your hooks at the bottom. I choose to use a french cleat to hang my storage shelf. Just make sure you are using studs and anchors in the walls to keep it secure.

steps.title: Visit www.MyLove2Create.com
steps.body: For more great project tutorials and building plans visit www.mylove2create.com!
